Research Projects

Featured Research Projects

Machine-Learned Force Fields for Graphene Oxide Interfaces

Developed two deep neural network force-fileds (DNFFs) with and without presence of excess protons to study structure and reactivity at graphene oxide–water interfaces using active learning. The DNFFs were trained on ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD) reference data and used to perform large-scale simulations of interfacial water structure, proton transfer, and vibrational sum-frequency generation spectra. A MACE potential was developed to simulate graphene oxide–methanol interfaces, to study proto transfer at the interface in longer timescales.

DeePMD MACE Query by Committee Ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD) Single-point DFT calculations LAMMPS ASE

Asphaltene Adsorption and Aggregation in Complex Solvent Environments

Investigated how asphaltene molecules organize, aggregate, and interact with surrounding solvent environments, using molecular dynamics simulations to compare solvent-dependent association, cluster formation, and interfacial behavior. The project connects atomistic configurations with larger questions of deposition, stability, and fouling in petroleum-relevant systems.

Impact: Links molecular-scale aggregation and adsorption mechanisms to enhanced oil recovery.

Classical MD Force-field parameterization PMF calculations Umbrella sampling Metadynamics Adsorption Aggregation MLIP UMA foundational model Trajectory analysis
